title: Success Story: Just 12 Days to NIW Success! Our Firm Helped a Nepal Staff Scientist Get Approved Swiftly with Premium Processing Service
content:
Client’s Testimonial:
“This is great news!! I feel very elated to have my NIW approved!!”
On June 9 th , 2025, we received another EB-2 NIW ( National Interest Waiver ) approval for a Staff Scientist in the Field of Computational Engineering ( Approval Notice ).
General Field: Computational Engineering
Position at the Time of Case Filing : Staff Scientist
Country of Origin: Nepal
State of Residence at the Time of Filing: North Carolina
Approval Notice Date: June 9 th , 2025
Processing Time: 12 days (Premium Processing Requested)
We are proud to share the approval of an I-140 National Interest Waiver ( NIW ) petition for a computational engineer whose pioneering work bridges civil engineering, revenue management, machine learning, and artificial intelligence (AI) to solve critical challenges in infrastructure resilience, revenue optimization, and public health.
Integrating AI and Statistical Modeling for National Impact
The client’s proposed endeavor involves developing scalable computational systems that integrate statistical modeling, machine learning, and AI to address pressing societal needs. His research supports advancements in water distribution systems (WDSs), public health, and large-scale revenue industries such as hospitality and tourism. By leveraging deep learning models and data-driven simulations, his work addresses nationwide issues like water system degradation and leakage, forecasting inefficiencies, and contamination detection—ultimately improving infrastructure monitoring and societal well-being.
He is currently employed as a staff scientist and has secured a position as a senior associate scientist at a leading analytics technology company. In this role, he develops cutting-edge AI algorithms for time-series forecasting and publishes peer-reviewed research to advance broader adoption of these tools in both engineering and commercial sectors.
Proven Academic and Scientific Contributions
At the time of filing, the petitioner had authored 2 peer-reviewed journal articles, 1 peer-reviewed conference article, and 1 filed patent application , and had accrued 20 citations , with one paper ranking among the top 10% most-cited articles in engineering for its year of publication. He also served as a reviewer for 8 manuscripts submitted to high-impact journals in the field, highlighting his recognized expertise.
The petition demonstrated that his algorithms for leak detection and sensor placement have been adopted by municipal water systems and cited globally in peer-reviewed studies, underscoring the broad applicability and influence of his work.
Expert Endorsements Support National Interest
To support the petition, 4 letters of recommendation from established experts in academia and industry were submitted. These experts praised the petitioner’s innovative methods and their real-world applications.
“Given the significance of water conservation and sustainability for preserving natural resources, ensuring long-term water security, and mitigating the impacts of climate change, it is evident that [Client’s] work is invaluable to the field. Thus, his research contributions make him an irreplaceable presence in the research community.”
With strong evidence of national relevance, academic success, and practical application, USCIS approved the petition just 12 days after filing under premium processing. We’re honored to support professionals advancing AI-driven solutions that benefit both the nation’s infrastructure and its economy.
--------------------------------------------
North America Immigration Law Group (Chen Immigration Law Associates) is a U.S. immigration law firm dedicated to representing corporations, research institutions, and individuals from all 50 U.S. states regarding I-140 immigration petitions. We specialize in employment-based immigration petition and have a proven record of high success rate for the categories of: EB2- NIW ( National Interest Waiver ), EB1-A ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ), EB1-B (Outstanding Researcher/Professor) and O-1 ( Alien of Extraordinary Ability ).
Our Sixty-four Thousand I-140 Approvals Provide Unprecedented Insight into the USCIS Adjudication Trend
With nearly 64,000 EB-1A , EB-1B , EB-2 NIW and O-1 cases approved, we have first hand information on the manner in which the USCIS adjudicate I-140 cases. As the USCIS has constantly changed its adjudication standards for the EB-1A , EB-1B and EB-2 NIW categories, our firm's huge database of successful cases gives you unprecedented insight to USCIS adjudication trends. We carefully analyze the data for all of our cases and apply the results of our analyses toward giving our clients up-to-date advice and adapting our strategies such that we remain on par with the ever-shifting landscape of immigration law in the U.S. With us, you will always have access to important updates, strategies, and information so that you can make the most informed decisions about your case.
We Have Helped Tens of Thousands of Clients with Credentials and Backgrounds Similar to Yours
With our exceedingly large number of successful petitions, no matter what credentials you have, no matter your background and field of expertise, no matter your visa status or nationality, chances are we have helped hundreds or even thousands of clients just like you. Our clients are usually impressed with how well we understand their research and work. Our insight and understanding stems from the fact that we have handled many cases with elements similar to yours already, and this helps us devise the best strategies for each individual petition.
Vast Majority of Clients Came to Us Because of Referrals
For years, our firm has attracted new clients based solely on word of mouth, recommendations, and the positive collaboration experiences shared with them by their friends and family. We take pride in our reputation and work hard to ensure that we provide a green card application experience that our clients are happy to share with their friends and colleagues. That is how our cumulative total of approved cases grew from 600 in 2013 to nearly 64,000 in 2025.
Track Record of Success EB-1 and NIW Approvals
